secondary_archetype: Technical Program Manager with localization/internationalization specialization

level_signals: 6+ years of experience in localization, international operations, or program/project management
Owns end-to-end localization program across all functions
Acts as strategic advisor as well as hands-on program manager

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 6+ years of experience in localization, international operations, or program/project management
Proven track record owning end-to-end localization programs across multiple languages and regions, ideally including building repeatable language or market launch processes
Strategic thinker who can zoom out to advise on market entry and zoom in to manage a process. You're equally comfortable in both modes
Experience working with TMS platforms (e.g., LILT, Phrase, Smartling) and a track record of owning and improving localization tooling and vendor relationships
Exceptional cross-functional skills. You know how to build trust, drive clarity, and move workstreams forward across Operations, Marketing, Design, Product, and GTM
Strong written and verbal communicator. Able to operate effectively in fast-moving, ambiguous environments

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in B2B SaaS or AI/tech companies
Familiarity with Figma, Sanity CMS, or similar design and content systems
Experience building localization programs from scratch at a scaling company

scope.surface: End-to-end localization program across product, marketing, design, and sales for global market expansion
primary_metrics_inferred: Localization coverage across languages and markets
Quality standards and QA pass rates
Turnaround time and vendor delivery performance
Timely alignment of localization with country expansion timelines
core_responsibilities: Contribute to the country expansion playbook: build and document repeatable processes, and coordinate execution across product, marketing, design, sales, and operations
Partner with GTM Ops to align localization priorities with country expansion plans and commercial timelines
Own and communicate the localization roadmap across all workstreams, keeping stakeholders aligned on priorities and timelines
Drive cross-functional program management across product UI, product documentation, website, sales assets, and digital campaigns
Manage our translation vendors and tooling - including turnaround expectations, quality standards, and delivery
Establish and own a QA framework across all localization projects, setting quality standards and ensuring consistency across markets and asset types
Coordinate localization rollout priorities with the marketing team and web producer across website, content, sales assets, and digital/search ads
Partner with Product, Engineering, and Design teams to define and integrate localization requirements into the product development lifecycle
Monitor and support internal localization systems and workflows, partnering with Engineering to troubleshoot issues, improve reliability, and maintain scalable translation operations

compensation.equity_form: Eligible full-time employees can participate in Sierra's equity plans subject to the terms of the applicable plans and policies.

green_flags: High-impact, clearly scoped role owning end-to-end localization at a well-funded AI startup with notable co-founders
Cross-functional reach across product, marketing, design, and GTM — broad influence
Company is actively expanding globally (multiple offices listed across multiple continents)
Encourages applicants even if experience doesn't precisely match the job description
red_flags: Role is explicitly based in San Francisco with no mention of remote or hybrid flexibility — eliminates non-SF candidates
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed in JD
No team size or reporting structure stated

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 6+ years requirement and scope of ownership)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2B AI platform)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly stated in JD)
location.type (inferred as onsite from 'primarily an in-person company' and 'Based in San Francisco' under role details)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as false from onsite signal)
ambiguities: The JD says Sierra is 'primarily an in-person company' and the role says 'Based in San Francisco', but no explicit hybrid/remote policy is stated for this specific role. Treated as onsite.

The nice-to-have 'ideally including building repeatable language or market launch processes' appears in the same bullet as a must-have requirement — the 'ideally' qualifier creates ambiguity; the broader bullet is classified as hard but the 'repeatable processes' sub-element is noted as preferred.
